name: Crystal grid lattice ob3ect: sacred geometry arrangement of crystals, each placed at a node of a geometric pattern (flower of life, platonic solid, or spiral) to amplify and direct subtle energy. FSPLIT: the central intention stone is activated, splitting its unified purpose through the lattice along geometric pathways to each peripheral crystal. FFUSE: the peripheral crystals reflect the energy back to the center, reconstituting the original intention now amplified by resonance. VINIT: the empty geometric template. TANCH: the outer boundary of the grid. AFWD: energy flowing from center to periphery. AREV: energy returning from periphery to center. ENGAGR: a crystal that is simultaneously a receiver (passive) and transmitter (active) — the quartz oscillator paradox.

phase_0.domain_name: Crystal grid lattice ob3ect: sacred geometry arrangement of crystals, each placed at a node of a geometric pattern (flower of life, platonic solid, or spiral) to amplify and direct subtle energy. FSPLIT: the central intention stone is activated, splitting its unified purpose through the lattice along geometric pathways to each peripheral crystal. FFUSE: the peripheral crystals reflect the energy back to the center, reconstituting the original intention now amplified by resonance. VINIT: the empty geometric template. TANCH: the outer boundary of the grid. AFWD: energy flowing from center to periphery. AREV: energy returning from periphery to center. ENGAGR: a crystal that is simultaneously a receiver (passive) and transmitter (active) — the quartz oscillator paradox.
phase_0.boundary_condition: the outer boundary of the grid (TANCH)

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any crystal is placed or intention set

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains and defines the entire crystal grid system

AFWD.chosen_element: energy flowing from center to periphery
AFWD.justification: forward morphism: directed transformation from central intention to peripheral crystals

AREV.chosen_element: energy returning from periphery to center
AREV.justification: reverse/descent morphism: energy flows back to the source

CLINK.chosen_element: sequential chaining of crystal activations along geometric pathways
CLINK.justification: composition: each activation step links to the next in sequence

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the central intention stone recognizing its own purpose
IMSCRIB.justification: identity/self-reference: the stone knows itself as the source

FSPLIT.chosen_element: central intention stone activated, splitting its unified purpose through the lattice
FSPLIT.justification: co-multiplication: one unified intention branches into multiple distinct energy paths to each peripheral crystal

FFUSE.chosen_element: peripheral crystals reflecting energy back to center, reconstituting original intention
FFUSE.justification: multiplication: the reflected energies merge to recover the original intention, now amplified

EVALT.chosen_element: grid resonating harmoniously with clear amplification
EVALT.justification: affirmative/success state: energy flows correctly and intention is amplified

EVALF.chosen_element: grid misaligned or blocked, energy dissipates
EVALF.justification: negative/failure state: intention is lost or scattered

ENGAGR.chosen_element: a crystal that is simultaneously a receiver and transmitter (quartz oscillator paradox)
ENGAGR.justification: both states simultaneously: passive and active, the paradox of bidirectional energy flow

IFIX.chosen_element: the permanent geometric pattern etched into the grid's memory
IFIX.justification: irreversible fixation: the arrangement is fixed and cannot be undone once activated

phase_3.void_description: empty geometric template before any crystal placed
phase_3.both_description: crystal acting as both receiver and transmitter simultaneously

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: central stone recognizes its own purpose
Step 2 AREV: energy descends from periphery to center to establish baseline
Step 3 FSPLIT: central intention splits into multiple paths to peripheral crystals
Step 4 AFWD: energy flows from center to periphery along geometric pathways
Step 5 FFUSE: peripheral crystals reflect energy back to center, reconstituting intention
Step 6 CLINK: sequential activation links each crystal in the geometric pattern
Step 7 IFIX: the grid pattern is permanently recorded in the lattice
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the grid recognizes itself as a completed system

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the geometric template that translates intention into crystal placements
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: resonant energy transfer between crystals along geometric pathways
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the etched pattern in the lattice storing the grid configuration
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the sequence of activation determined by the geometric pattern
phase_5.alfs_store: the canonical flower of life or platonic solid pattern as bootstrap store

phase_6.cycle_cost: intention energy consumed per activation cycle
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: grid in equilibrium, no active flow
phase_6.post_cycle_state: grid in equilibrium after amplified intention returned
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — energy is conserved and amplified through resonance, not dissipated
